metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify AC3: either confirm these test cases already exist (and name them), or add a 'What' step describing the two new test scenarios with concrete inputs and expected suppression/fire outcomes so an agent can author and verify them without guessing.
findings.finding: AC3 does not state whether the two 'scale-direction' recall test cases must be authored as part of this ticket or already exist in the test file; an executing agent cannot determine whether to write new tests or merely run existing ones, and if writing is required, no inputs, prompts, or expected outcomes are specified for the cases.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC3: 'Eval covers BOTH directions … two recall cases in tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py -k scale'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'What' section: 'Add two pytest cases under tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py with the marker scale: (1) a high-scale/high-blast fixture that must still fire at the reviewer's original severity, and (2) an inflated-scale fixture (grandiose subject, minor defect) that must be downgraded by the new anchors.' Then AC3 becomes verifiable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a work item in the 'What' section explicitly describing the two new eval test cases required by AC[3] (scale-suppression and scale-recall in tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py) so the test deliverable is covered in the plan body, not only in the acceptance criteria.
findings.finding: AC[3] requires adding two new recall test cases in tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py (scale-suppression + scale-recall), but the plan body's 'What' section describes only prompt/doc edits and never mentions modifying or creating test files — leaving AC[3] as a criterion with no corresponding described work item.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC[3] vs. What section
findings.suggested_fix: Under 'What (concrete edit locations)', add a bullet: '- tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py: add two recall cases (parametrized or separate) tagged -k scale — one asserting genuine high-scale/high-blast findings still fire, one asserting inflated-scale findings are suppressed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit step in the 'What' section (or a dedicated 'Test changes' subsection) that names the two eval recall cases to be added/updated in tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py: one asserting genuine high-scale/high-blast findings still fire, one asserting inflated-scale findings get suppressed.
findings.finding: The plan body (the 'What' section) describes only prompt/doc edits, but AC item 3 requires two new eval recall cases in tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py (-k scale). No step in the plan covers writing or updating these test cases, leaving a non-deferred goal without a faithful implementation step.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria item 3 vs. 'What (concrete edit locations)' section
findings.suggested_fix:
Under 'What (concrete edit locations)' add a fourth bullet:
- tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py: add two -k scale recall cases — (a) a genuine high-blast finding that must still fire after the anchors land, and (b) an inflated-scale input that must be suppressed. These gate the AC-3 grep proof.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit step in the 'What' section to write two new test cases in `tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py` keyed `-k scale` — one scale-suppression case (inflated scale gets lowered) and one genuine high-scale recall case — so AC[3]'s proof command has something to run green.
findings.finding: AC[3] asserts 'tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py -k scale' will run green with two new recall cases (scale-suppression + genuine high-scale), but the plan's 'What' section describes ONLY prompt/doc edits (A1.md, T5a.md, verifier*.md, verify.py) and never mentions writing any test code. Reading test_plan_review_evals.py confirms no 'scale' test exists today. The delivery path for the required test cases is entirely absent from the plan body — an unverifiable assertion.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'What' section: 'tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py: add test_scale_suppression_fires and test_genuine_high_scale_recall (pytest mark: scale) — two canned-response eval cases using the existing mocked-LLM harness, one confirming inflated scale is suppressed by the new A1/T5a anchors, one confirming a genuinely system-wide finding still fires.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py` to the 'What (concrete edit locations)' list, specifying the two new scale-direction test cases (one recall: genuine high-scale/high-blast finding fires; one suppression: inflated-scale finding is downgraded) required by AC[3].
findings.finding: tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py is absent from the 'What (concrete edit locations)' section, yet AC[3] requires two new test cases (scale-recall and scale-suppression, -k scale) be added to it. A search for 'scale' in that file returns no matches, confirming these cases are CREATION (new behavior). This is an unenumerated edit target.
findings.suggested_fix: Under 'What (concrete edit locations)', add: `- tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py: add two parametrized cases under a marker or id containing 'scale' — one recall case (a genuine system-wide finding still fires) and one suppression case (a grand-subject finding with a small-scope flaw gets blast_radius=local).`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Draft the exact canonical phrases for 'small-scale default', 'prohibited-reasoning examples', and 'two-directional bar' that both plan_review_A1.md and plan_review_T5a.md must contain, so AC[1]'s grep is deterministic.
findings.finding: The plan names plan_review_A1.md and plan_review_T5a.md as edit targets for 'small-scale default + prohibited-reasoning examples + two-directional bar' text, but never drafts or quotes the new text. AC[1] requires a grep proof against specific phrases, yet those phrases appear nowhere in the plan — an implementer has no authoritative text to insert and cannot satisfy the AC without inventing content.
findings.location: What section / AC[1]: plan_review_A1.md and plan_review_T5a.md
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Canonical anchor text' subsection to the plan's What section quoting the literal strings to insert, e.g.: small-scale default: 'Most findings concern a LOCAL, SMALL-SCALE flaw — default blast_radius to local unless the evidence explicitly shows cross-cutting reach.'; prohibited-reasoning: 'DO NOT inflate severity because the plan's subject matter is large or architecturally important.'; two-directional bar: 'Calibrate in BOTH directions: genuine system-wide findings must still fire; inflated-scale findings must be suppressed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the 'What' section an explicit work item for AC[3]: specify the two new eval-spec dataset entries (scale-recall and scale-suppression cases with their mode/expect/criterion fields) to add to the plan-review-verifier or plan-review-finder eval spec, and the corresponding test function(s) with the 'scale' pytest marker that asserts their presence.
findings.finding: AC[3] requires two new test cases in tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py matching '-k scale' (one genuine-high-scale recall case, one inflated-scale suppression case), but the plan's 'What' section describes only prompt/doc edits — adding test cases is unmentioned as a work item. The test file currently has zero scale-related tests and the plan-review-verifier eval spec dataset has no 'scale' mode cases. Without specifying what eval-spec dataset entries these tests validate against, the AC is structurally incomplete.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in AC3 which eval spec(s) receive the two scale cases (e.g. plan-review-finder for the inflation-suppression case and plan-review-verifier for the genuine-high-scale recall case), so the -k scale Python test function can be written with a concrete named spec to query.
findings.finding: AC3 does not specify which eval spec file (plan-review-finder.eval.yaml, plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml, or a new one) should receive the two scale recall/suppression cases, making the test implementation path underspecified: the existing test_plan_review_evals.py pattern (e.g. test_finder_has_t12_discrimination_pair) always names a specific spec, and the -k scale Python test function cannot be written without knowing which spec to query.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC3 — 'two recall cases in tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py -k scale'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC3: 'Add one `mode: inflated-scale` false-fire case (expect: pass) to plan-review-finder.eval.yaml and one genuine high-scale recall case (expect: finding, mode: genuine-high-scale) to plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml; the -k scale test asserts both cases exist in their respective specs.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the plan the exact sentence to add to (a) both verifier prompt blast_radius descriptions and (b) the SeverityAttrs.blast_radius Field description in verify.py, so the 'never raises' grep AC is deterministic and the rule is co-located with the schema field that needs it.
findings.finding: The 'blast_radius only LOWERS, never raises' ratchet rule is described in the plan as a processing decision rule to add to both verifier prompts and the SeverityAttrs.blast_radius doc in verify.py, but the plan never specifies the exact phrasing. The SeverityAttrs schema (verify.py:116) defines the enum vocabulary but carries no co-located ratchet rule; the plan's AC[2] grep proof ('never raises') depends on a literal string the plan itself never quotes.
findings.location: What section / AC[2]: plan_review_verifier.md, plan_review_verifier_agentic.md, SeverityAttrs.blast_radius in src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify.py:116
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the SeverityAttrs.blast_radius Field description: 'local|module|system — RATCHET: blast_radius can only LOWER severity, never raise it; a grand subject does not widen a local flaw.' Mirror the same sentence in both verifier prompt blast_radius bullets.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one AC that exercises the changed prompts end-to-end against the live LLM (e.g., a smoke eval or integration test), or explicitly document why offline/mocked unit tests are sufficient to prove prompt-level calibration changes.
findings.finding: The only end-to-end proof that the new calibration anchors actually alter LLM reviewer reasoning is a unit-test run under tests/unit/, which is almost certainly backed by mocked/offline LLM responses that bypass the live prompt path — no AC exercises the changed prompts against a real LLM call.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC3 + 'What' section: 'these are prompt/doc edits'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a live/end-to-end acceptance criterion (e.g., a named integration or eval test that calls the real LLM with the new prompts) to confirm the calibration anchors actually suppress inflated-scale output in practice, rather than relying solely on tests/unit/ which cannot exercise the LLM boundary.
findings.finding: The sole test evidence for the new LLM-prompt calibration behavior points to tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py, but unit tests almost certainly use mocked/canned LLM responses that bypass the actual LLM boundary where the calibration change lives. New prompt text that anchors reviewer reasoning can only be validated by exercising the real LLM with the new prompts; if the unit suite runs against stubs, it goes green regardless of whether the actual LLM follows the new instructions — a textbook proxy-validation failure for a changed-default path.
findings.location: AC #3 — 'Proof: two recall cases in tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py -k scale; run it green'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: 'An integration/eval test (tests/evals/ or tests/integration/) makes a live call with a known over-firing plan fragment and asserts the new prompt suppresses inflated severity; also asserts a legitimate high-scale finding still fires. This test is permitted to be skipped in offline CI but must pass in the nightly eval run.' This ensures at least one green signal from the actual boundary.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
